phase_note: Phase 1 adds a runnable named-argument and default-value baseline while the manifest inventories duplicate-name, ordering, and vararg precedents.

cases.upstream_focus: Named-call syntax must reject duplicated keyword delivery and mixed unpacking collisions.
cases.zr_decision: ZR named arguments are accepted only when each parameter name is bound at most once across positional, named, and future spread forms.

cases.upstream_focus: Defaults and required named-only parameters need both positive and negative coverage.
cases.zr_decision: ZR defaults are applied only to omitted parameters, and later tests should add missing-required-name diagnostics.

cases.upstream_focus: Call behavior needs stable handling for unexpected names, keyword ordering, and keyword-capable vs keyword-forbidden callables.
cases.zr_decision: ZR should preserve the explicit source order of named arguments while resolving them against parameter names, not by textual coincidence alone.

cases.upstream_focus: Once named arguments appear, ordering mistakes and unused names need dedicated diagnostics rather than generic parse failures.
cases.zr_decision: ZR later tests should reject positional-after-named and unused or unknown named arguments with targeted compiler errors.

cases.upstream_focus: Call coverage should include flexible arity entry points rather than assuming every callable has a fixed positional shape.
cases.zr_decision: ZR keeps vararg-style and named/default semantics as separate test themes so later broadening does not distort the current callable contract.

cases.upstream_focus: General call semantics need a broad executable baseline to catch regressions in argument passing and nested invocation shape.
cases.zr_decision: ZR phase 1 adds a concrete named/default fixture that returns a stable value and will expand from this baseline.
